title: Common Faults — MacBook Pro 13" M1 (A2338, 820-02020)
WARNING: A2338 is MBP 13" M1. Not MBA. Has Touch Bar, fan, 2x USB-C, no MagSafe. SVR AON is 36A.
data_source: Schematic PNGs (pages 21-22, 25, 28, 32, 37, 57) cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (35 A2338 devices, 22 with logic board work)

saf_statistics.a2338_vs_a2337: Liquid damage is 3x more prevalent on A2338 (41%) vs A2337 (13%). Touch Bar connector is a unique A2338 liquid damage hotspot. CPU/T2 swaps documented on A2338 but not A2337.

faults.symptom: No power at all — no LED, no fan spin, no PPBUS_AON
faults.likely_cause: Charger circuit failure, FF200/FF201 blown, or battery disconnect
diagnosis: Check FF200/FF201 fuses (page 55) — blown fuse = short downstream
Check J5150 solder pads (page 21) — BMU flex is SOLDERED, can crack
Check SYS_DETECT circuit (Q5155, S5190/S5191)
Check U5200 charger IC (353S01525) for shorts (page 22)
Check PPOCIN_USBC_AON for voltage when charger connected
Check CD3217 port controllers UF400/UF500 (pages 57-58)

examples: BM 1016: 5V/0.023A — 'lots of liquid damage, clan and repair all liquid damage parts touch bar connector CD chips'. Fault: Liquid Damage.
BM 1234: 5V — 'macbook not on and working, logic board has liquid damage, repair logic board and charging port'. Fault: Liquid Damage, Charging IC, Audio IC.
BM 1350: 5V (ammeter mislabeled, actual 20V/2.5A) — 'logic board has liquid damage'. Fault: Liquid Damage, Battery, Keyboard.
saf_data.pattern: 5V low-current on A2338 strongly correlates with liquid damage (100% of cases). Clean board first.

faults.likely_cause: U5700 SVR AON failure (36A) or enable signal not reaching it
diagnosis: Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN — is it asserted? (delayed by U5340 circuit, page 23)
Check P3V8AON_FAULT_L — if LOW, fault condition in U5700 (page 25)
Check TS5060 temperature sensor (page 25)
Check for short on PP3V8_AON rail — thermal image the board
Common: short on PP3V8_AON VDDMAIN distribution (page 100)
Check phase driver MOSFETs (Q5800 area, page 26)

examples: BM 940: 'one ic explosion on logic board (u8250) after we replace ic but still same, then two CD chis issue'. U8250 is near PP3V8_AON distribution.
BM 1166: 20V/0.8A — Short Circuit + T2 Chip + LCD fault keywords.
saf_data.pattern: IC explosion (U8250) is a documented A2338 failure near PP3V8_AON area. Visual inspection for burnt components critical.

faults.likely_cause: UC300 (LT8642S) failure or enable signal missing
diagnosis: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal
Check PP5V2_PGOOD power good (page 37)
Check UC300 (LT8642S) input PPBUS_AON — 5.15V/6.6A/1.5MHz output
Check LC329 inductor (CRITICAL) for open circuit (page 37)
Check XMC332 sense resistor
Check 5VS2 discharge circuit: QC420 MOSFET, RC423/RC422 bleeders (page 38)
Check 5VS2_EN turn off delay circuit: DC401, RC401/RC402 (page 38)

faults.symptom: No display but board boots (fan spins, SSD activity)
faults.likely_cause: Display power sequencer, BEN controller, or EDP line fault
diagnosis: Check LCD_PWR_EN signal at UP700 (SLG4AP4365V, page 69)
Check PMU_SYS_ALIVE at UP700 pin 3
Check PP5V_SW_LCD and PP3V3_SW_LCD from UP701
Check PPVOUT_LCDBKLT from UP800 BEN (page 70)
Check JP600 display connector for damage/corrosion (page 68)
Check display flex cable seating
Check EDP lines from SoC — SAF: LCD line short from CPU documented

examples: BM 1166: 20V/0.8A — 'one LCD line short (EDP from CPU) after T2 swap macbook working fine'. Fault: CPU Issue, Short Circuit, T2 Chip, LCD.
BM 1051: 'screen image not coming, has been repair before logic board, Camera line has one FL610 and make new jumper'.
saf_data.pattern: EDP line short from CPU is a documented A2338 fault requiring T2/SoC rework. Camera flex line (FL610) jumper repair also documented.

faults.likely_cause: DFR power rail failure or DFR connector liquid damage
diagnosis: Check DER_PWR_EN signal
Check PP1V8_AWAKE_DFR from UT700 (page 86/87)
Check PP3V3_AWAKE_DFR from UT710
Check DFR touch connector JT700 (page 86)
Check DFR display connector JT710 (page 86)
Check SPI_DFR signals and level shifters (UT820/UT830/UT860/UT880/UT890)

examples: BM 1016: 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, clan and repair all liquid damage parts touch bar connector CD chips'. Then later: 'repair touch bar and working fine' and 'found touch bar plat making issue we repair new plat'.
BM 1209: 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, we repair new connector and some liquid damage parts change on logic board and touch bar working fine'.
saf_data.pattern: Touch Bar connector is a UNIQUE A2338 liquid damage hotspot. A2337 doesn't have DFR. Inspect JT700/JT710 connectors under microscope for corrosion.

diagnosis: Check PP5V_S2_TO_FAN at JE501 connector (page 52)
Check QE500 (NTS4C569N) MOSFET — drives fan via SMC_FAN_LS_PWM
Check UE501 level shifter (SN74A4C1T45)
Check fan connector JE501 for damage
Check FAN_RT_TACH signal — if fan connected but no tach, motor seized
Check thermal sensor connections (TDEV pages 49-50)

examples: BM 1173: 'after repair logic board we found fan sound lowly after we found near fan connector has Two ic has issue'.
saf_data.pattern: ICs near fan connector can fail on A2338. Liquid damage path from keyboard can reach fan connector area.

faults.symptom: USB-C port not working (one port only)
faults.likely_cause: CD3217 or retimer failure on affected port
diagnosis: Identify port: ATC0 = left rear (debug), ATC1 = left front
Check CD3217: UF400 (ATC0, page 57) or UF500 (ATC1, page 58)
Check retimer: UF000 (ATC0) or UF100 (ATC1) on pages 53-54
Check port-specific power: PP3V3_S2_UPC, PP1V25_S2
Check CC lines for shorts (USBC0_CC1/CC2 or USBC1_CC1/CC2)
Check USB2 chokes: LF800 (ATC0) or LF810 (ATC1) on page 61

examples: BM 1199: 'repair logic board all liquid damage parts three ic and 6 ragiester and keyboard and charging port'. Charging port = USB-C connector/controller.
BM 1234: 'repair logic board and charging port'. Charging IC + Liquid Damage.
BM 1350: 'USB-C Port' in parts list alongside logic board repair.
saf_data.pattern: USB-C port/charging port repair is in 14% of A2338 cases. Often co-occurs with liquid damage and keyboard faults.

faults.likely_cause: Rasputin module or antenna connection failure
diagnosis: Check WLBT_PWR_EN signal
Check PP1V25_S2 and PP3V3_S2 at Rasputin module (page 62)
Check PP3V3_S2_WLBT_ISNS, PP1V8_S2_WLBT_ISNS rails
Check antenna connectors JL110-JL133 (SUNWAY, page 63)
Check PCIE WLAN/BT reset and clock signals
Rasputin module (LBRECAUV1-506) is soldered — rework requires BGA
Check PPBUS_SPKRAMP_LEFT_ISNS and PPBUS_SPKRAMP_RIGHT_ISNS (page 78)
Check PP1V25_AWAKE_IO and PP1V8_AWAKE at amp ICs (pages 76-77)
Check UR500 (CS42L83) audio codec (page 75)
Check speaker amp connectors JR800 (left) and JR850 (right) (page 78)
4 active amps: A/B left (UR600 area), D/E right (UR700 area)
Check SPKRAMP_LVS_RESET_L signals

examples: BM 1234: 'try try to repair audio ic other parts logic board but still same no improve on sound. No point to spent more time please QC completed like minor'. Audio IC: Liquid Damage, Charging IC also present.
saf_data.pattern: Audio IC failure on A2338 may be UNREPAIRABLE. SAF shows attempted repair with no success. Assess cost/benefit before attempting board-level audio IC rework.

faults.likely_cause: Keyboard IOX, connector, liquid damage, or flex damage
diagnosis: Check PP3V3_AON_KBD_CONN rail
Check PP1V85_S2_KBD_ISNS (keyboard power sense)
Check keyboard IOX1 (UT101, PCA6416A) and IOX2 (UT102) on page 81
Check UT103 (BUMQU03V) RSLOC isolation IC
Check JT200 membrane ZIF connector (30-pin, page 82)
Check keyboard ESD protection (DZT201-DZT206, page 82)

examples: BM 1261: 20V/2.5A — 'change new keyboard but keyboard back light not good. we used pull track pad and keyboard back light and bezel'.
BM 1335: 20V/2.5A — 'keyboard has liquid damage and track pad liquid damage. repair new keyboard and track pad'.
BM 1144: 'repair new keyboard and full screen also keyboard back light. because it's chaina keyboard'.
BM 997: 'keyboard has liquid damage and sticky'.
BM 1241: 'repair new keyboard and keyboard back light very minor low keyboard back light right side'.
saf_data.pattern: Keyboard is the DOMINANT fault on A2338 — 64% of logic board cases. Keyboard backlight shared A2289/A2338 part. Liquid damage frequently causes keyboard failure. Right side backlight dimness is a recurring post-repair issue.

faults.likely_cause: NAND power rail failure or controller fault
diagnosis: Check PP2V5_AWAKE_NAND (SPMU BUCK 6, page 28/100)
Check PP1V25_AWAKE5W_VCCQ (SPMU BUCK 13 SW5)
Check PP0V88_AWAKE5W_NAND (SPMU BUCK 12 SW6)
Check NAND0 reset: NAND0_RESET_L, NAND0_PCIE_RESET_L (page 67)
Check PMU_SYS_ALIVE → NAND0_PEN_L path (RP002, page 67)
Check non-Ocarina VCC discharge QN420 (page 66)
NAND is soldered — paired to SoC, not user-replaceable

faults.likely_cause: Trackpad power, SPI bus, or connector fault
diagnosis: Check PP5V_S2_TPAD_ISNS (via LT400 ferrite, page 84)
Check PP3V3_S2_TPAD (UT305 LDO, page 83)
Check PP1V85_S2_IPD (UT300 LDO, page 83)
Check IPD_PWR_EN signal (AND gate on page 83)
Check JT400 trackpad connector (DF40C-50DS, page 84)
Check SPI TPAD signals and level shifter UT360 (page 83)

examples: BM 1335: 'track pad liquid damage'. Keyboard + trackpad liquid damage together.
BM 1239: 'swap logic board and change new track pad and flex'.
saf_data.pattern: Trackpad failure often co-occurs with keyboard liquid damage on A2338.

faults.likely_cause: Corrosion on critical power or signal paths
diagnosis: Clean board with isopropyl alcohol and ultrasonic cleaner FIRST
Inspect all connector areas under microscope — focus on Touch Bar connector JT700/JT710
Inspect fan connector JE501 area — SAF: ICs near fan fail
Inspect hall sensor area — SAF: liquid damage near hall sensor documented
Check for corroded 0201 resistors near PMU (pages 28-35)
Check PP3V8_AON distribution — corrosion can cause partial shorts
After cleaning: run full power-on sequence from diagnostic tree Step 1

examples: BM 1114: 5V/0.2A — 'very badly liquid damage' — CPU swap required. Most extreme repair.
BM 1173: 5V — 'repair logic board liquid damage all parts and ic. near fan connector Two ic has issue'.
BM 1211: 20V/0.6A — 'CD chis ROM and some CD chis line make jumper then macbook working fine'. 1TB/16GB board saved.
BM 1276: 20V/2.5A — 'near hall sensor ic has liquid damage we repair and make one jumper'.
BM 1126: 'repair logic board liquid damage parts and change new battery'.
saf_data.pattern: Liquid damage is 41% of A2338 cases (vs 13% on A2337). Touch Bar connector, fan connector area, and hall sensor area are A2338-specific liquid damage hotspots. CD3217 ROM corruption + jumper wires is a viable repair path. CPU swap is last resort for severe cases.

diagnosis: Visual inspection for burnt/exploded components
SAF documented: U8250 explosion near MPMU area
After replacing exploded IC: check for secondary damage to surrounding passives
Check for CD3217 co-failure — SAF: U8250 explosion + two CD chips failure
Full diagnostic tree after repair to verify no other failures

examples: BM 940: 'one ic explosion on logic board (u8250) after we replace ic but still same macbook not taking AMM but on and working. Post repair: repair logic board and new battery we found explosion on logic board and two CD chips issue'.
saf_data.pattern: U8250 explosion is a documented A2338 failure. Board was repairable after replacing U8250 + two CD chips. Always check for multi-component failure when IC explosion is found.
